instruction.title: Prepare the meat mix
steps: Place the minced meat in a large chilled bowl.
Add grated onion (squeezed dry), ginger-garlic paste, green chili, coriander, and mint.
Add all ground spices, salt, and roasted gram flour.
Mix well with your hands for at least 8–10 minutes until the mixture becomes sticky (this ensures binding).

instruction.title: Resting
steps: Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour (preferably overnight). This helps flavors develop and mixture firm up.

instruction.title: Shaping the kebabs
steps: Lightly oil your hands and skewers.
Take a portion of the mix and mold it around a skewer, pressing gently to form a long cylinder (approx. 6–8 inches).
Ensure the kebabs are even in thickness for uniform cooking.

instruction.title: Cooking
steps: Tandoor/Grill: Cook directly over high heat, rotating until evenly charred and cooked (8–10 minutes).
Oven Method: Preheat oven to 240°C (475°F). Place kebabs on a greased rack over a tray and cook for 12–15 minutes, flipping midway. Finish with 2–3 minutes under the broiler for char.

instruction.title: Optional Smoky Finish (Dhungar):
steps: Heat a piece of charcoal until red-hot.
Place it in a small steel bowl inside the kebab tray, pour 1 tsp ghee over it, and immediately cover with a lid for 2 minutes.
This gives the kebabs a restaurant-style smoky aroma.
Serve hot with mint-coriander chutney, sliced onions, lemon wedges, and butter naan.
Garnish with a light sprinkle of chaat masala before serving.
